analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

He is so enthusiastic and knowledgeable..you won't want to miss a class.  Second exam seems to be the hardest....study a lot and dont buy the book, its only good for the examples because his notes are so detailed and all on the board.

And the exams are most of the class grade, and you will not be prepared though any provided materials.

The quizzes he gives are also difficult although you do get to work with everyone on them if you want. The main issue with the quizzes are the way he words things.

very clear, awesome teacher...he should run the lab portion of the class.  he explains everythign in detail, is  very interested in helping.  go over the problems he has on carmen over, and over, and over again to do well on exams.  puts bonues questions on exams.  there is NO CURVE in this class.  class average was a 66%, no CURVE...weed-out class

I got a C overall in the course but even I realize Marc is an amazing professor, you either like him or hate him there is no in between. I am personally taking the second required accounting course at CSCC, simply because 78% of your final grade is exam weight. In short, if you are good at math, this course should be alright.

analysis.name: Patricia Muir

This class will provide you with information which will serve you well into your career.  The preparation of material is meticulously selected to provide the student with everything they'll need.  Prof. Muir is extremely dedicated to each student doing their best and I'm so glad that I had her for my first semester.

I absolutely love Patty! She is so kind and considerate! Her directions on assignments are a little vague at times so I would ask clarifying questions. She loves to answer your questions so don't be afraid to ask!
Patti is wonderful. Lots of homework, but you always know exactly what is expected. Extremely organized.

analysis.name: Zakaria Nyongesa

He is one of the best professors on the campus! You can make a good friendship with him even after you move on to more advanced classes! He is such a joy! Swahili is an easy language to learn and he is a great professor for it!

Knowledge is in the mind of the creator. We are only willing to see what we are capable of. We can only learn with an open mind.  Professor Nyongesa is a rare jem. analysis.name: Nathan Wallace
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Even as an English major this class was more than I expected, but I learned a lot and Wallace is willing to help you understand and is willing to work with you. Discussion based with questions from the homework. Reading could be complicated, but you always talked about it in class. Nice Professor who is very passionate about what he teaches.

Aside from his Shakespeare class, which is the most difficult class out of all of those he teaches, he is a wonderful professor! If you like writing, he is great to talk with and share your work with! His assigned materials may be hard to understand (he's a Joycean scholar, so that makes sense) and may not be engaging, but he is great!

Class was discussion-based which is ok because Wallace is great at getting everyone to contribute without pressure. Get ready for TONS of homework. Usually 20 pages minimum, over long weekends he'll give more like all of Othello. Also, you need to type answers to questions posted on Carmen every night, usually a page or longer. Hard, but rewarding.

Be prepared to watch multiple Shakespeare plays and actively participate in class. I found it difficult to keep on top of assignments when he tells you about them almost last minute. He grades things a bit weird and not on a consistent schedule. Lectures either stressed me out or made me want to fall asleep. Interesting information though.

I loved this class!  Dr. Wallace is a great professor who tries to make his class as enjoyable, engaging, and fun as possible.  The class does contain a work load at times, but I promise that it does pay off.  I have very rarely taken a class and walked away feeling that I had learned this much.  Would highly recommend taking this class!

analysis.name: Travis Neel

Overall, I would highly recommend him.

Travis was awesome! He was so enthusiastic about the material and it was evident how much he loved teaching. He made discussions interesting and wasn't too tough when it came to grading.  I would definitely recommend taking his class!

I took Travis' english class at OSU Columbus. He was absolutely excellent. I am a Bio major and not very into English, but I grew to love his Shakespeare class. He is very helpful, passionate, respectful, and kind. I recommend any possible course offered by Dr. Neel. Treated students with a kindness you don't receive from most teachers. Exceptional

Incredibly unorganized. The class seemed to have no direction. Did not receive a single grade back until the last week of school! He is very enthusiastic about the topic, but he will ramble on without actually saying anything. Although I was very interested in the topic, I felt as though this class was a waste of my time.

If you ever get this professor for class, consider yourself absurdly lucky. He is absolutely passionate about what he is teaching and completely hilarious. Material is not challenging if you actually pay attention. A lot of people skipped class and sent each other prelabs/worksheets, they didn't do well. Dr. T is kind and caring but her lectures are very boring.

This class is an entry level engineering class. You'll spend the first half learning excel, and second half learning the basics of MATLAB. The teacher was mediocre, but that's because the material she was forced by the university to teach was kind of bland. Just do your homework in the class, and really take your time to learn the MATLAB portion.

Dr. Tahmina is great, take her! Responds to emails quickly and is always willing to help. You must attend class or you will fall behind though. Lectures can be boring but they are crucial. Graded on 10 online quizzes (easy), 10 pre-labs (pretty easy), and in-class labs (somewhat difficult, but you can always ask for help), and 3 exams (hard).

analysis.name: Roger Williams

analysis.name: Ryan Yoder
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

review_highlights.comment: Dr.Yoder is absolutely amazing! OSUM was very lucky to get him. He is an excellent instructor and explains the material very well. He is tough but extremely fair. Be aware that he does have specific grading criteria, so make sure you understand all aspects of the questions. Quizzes are medium in difficulty and he steps it up for the exams.

Dr. Yoder is by far the best professor I have ever had in my college career. You can truly tell how much he loves his job and cares for his students. If you are struggling with content, he is very available outside of class and always willing to help! Ohio State is truly lucky to have him. I could not recommend a professor more than Dr. Yoder!

Very lecture heavy. Extremely recommended you do not skip class, though attendance is not graded. If you pay attention during class you should be fine. Quizzes and exams are geared towards your specific class and what you may struggle with. Class is heavily curved. Very accessible in person and email. Explains things very well.

Yoder makes OCHEM 1 and 2 reasonable. I am thankful that I could take it both semesters with him due to the online format. Way better than the other OSU professors. Reasonable homework and quizzes. The exams step it up, but if you put in the work you can succeed. Good professor.

analysis.name: Tiffiny Rye-McCurdy

Her office hours and STEM Center hours are invaluable and help further content understanding.

Dr. Tiff is by far the best professor I have ever encountered in college. Organic chemistry is hard, so you will have to work for your grade, but Tiff made this content significantly easier. She is not only a great professor, but an incredibly caring person. I could not recommend a professor more.

Dr. Tiff is one of the best professors at OSU. She cares a lot about her students and understands that OChem is a difficult subject. She gives bonus before every midterm and the final and is very generous with curves. 11/10 would take Dr. Tiff!

analysis.name: Robert Zellmer

If you go into his office hours, he can be a wealth of knowledge, prepare for alot of work!

He is a very nice professor. He doesn't make you feel stupid for any questions that you ask. He always leaves time at the end of class if you need help with anything or have anymore questions. He likes to joke around and makes sure you understand the content. He always shows different ways to work the problems so you learn best.
Most amazing professor. Understanding and wants you to succeed. He doesn't hide anything in he syllabus adn is upfront with all his students. He is so sweet and caring and deserves the world.

This course was very difficult the way it was set up. The lecture slides on Canvas were there for you to access but they did not have the steps to show you how to do the problems. Professor Sawicki is a knowledgeable and gifted lecturer. She has a firm grasp of the subject matter and knows how to present it coherently. I hope I am able to take another one of her classes.

Hands down THE best professor here at OSU Marion. Her class was the highlight of my semester, and as long as you do your work and study, you'll do amazing in this class.
